Asha Hingorani Michel Guay Provide a description of the organization's business or activities. Formed in 1988 to promote the benefits of peat to horticulturists and home gardeners throughout North America, the Canadian Sphagnum Peat Moss Association (CSPMA) is comprised of 17 peat moss producers and marketers representing 96 percent of Canada's total production. In Alberta currently there are 5 companies with active interest in development within the province. Canada, the world's second largest peat producer and exporter of sphagnum peat moss for horticultural use, produces more than 99 percent of the sphagnum peat moss imported by the United States. The association has a strict environmental policy that regulates the way peat bogs are harvested and restored. There are no Alberta government supported projects on-going at the present time.
